title: Latent tyre-state filter instead of more static degradation ratios
summary: Recent F1 tire modeling work uses a state-space model with pit stops as state resets, which matches this task better than hand-set life thresholds.
rationale: Recent F1 tire modeling work uses a state-space model with pit stops as state resets, which matches this task better than hand-set life thresholds. A per-driver, within-race latent wear/pace state could capture nonlinear stint collapse that current fixed expected-life features miss.
implementation_hint: Build leakage-safe sequential features per Race-Year-Driver using only prior laps: exponentially weighted residual pace, degradation slope/intercept, one-step-ahead residual surprise, and a 'state reset confidence' after PitStop. Treat pit stops as reset points and estimate separate smooth/volatile wear states for slick vs wet compounds.
expected_effect: Should add complementary signal on imminent stint failure, especially when TyreLife alone is ambiguous or compounds behave atypically.
risk: Easy to leak if fitted with future laps from the same stint; keep all filter/state updates strictly causal within each row.
